title: GHL CMMC Gap Assessment Workflow v1
SBA CMMC Roundtable Follow-Up — David Mullis (Office of Advocacy)
content:
Rick sent a follow-up email to David Mullis (SBA Office of Advocacy) after the CMMC Small Business Impacts Roundtable held March 12, 2026.

KEY POINTS:
- SBA hosted a roundtable on CMMC impacts on small businesses. Huge response/interest.
- David Mullis wants continued feedback on CMMC impact and offered one-on-one calls.
- Rick positioned CGA as the non-profit solving the cost/complexity barrier for SMBs in the defense supply chain.
- Highlighted CGA's funded gap assessments, pen testing grants, and the 250 free CyberCert certifications.
- Requested a 20 min call with David in the next couple weeks.

CONTACT:
- David Mullis, SBA Office of Advocacy (david.mullis@sba.gov)
- SBA Office of Advocacy is the independent voice for small business in the executive branch, created by Congress in 1976.

ATTACHMENTS:
- SBA Roundtable Follow-Up Email (data/sba-cmmc-roundtable-followup-david-mullis.pdf)
- SBA CMMC Roundtable Presentation (data/sba-cmmc-roundtable-presentation-march2026.pdf)
- GAO CMMC Report (referenced in email, not attached)

ACTION ITEMS:
1. Follow up if no response within 1 week
2. Prepare talking points for the one-on-one call with David
3. Gather SMB barrier data to share with SBA Office of Advocacy

STRATEGIC VALUE: Direct relationship with SBA Office of Advocacy could influence CMMC policy in favor of small businesses and position CGA as a key stakeholder in the conversation.

title: CGA Grant Pipeline — 14 Leads Prioritized (March 17, 2026)
content:

   Active IDIQ contracts, 8 CUI categories, DCAA compliant, GSA Schedule. AWAITING ZOOM SCHEDULING.
